skill.name: longbridge-derivatives
skill.description: Analyze options chains, quotes, volume, Greeks, implied volatility, and Hong Kong warrants using Longbridge market data.
skill.summary: Analyze options and warrant data from Longbridge for HK and US markets.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns, but most are Markdown command examples or inline code references for the Longbridge CLI. The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and environment access hits are false positives from options terminology and prose. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscated code, or destructive command intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Documented Longbridge CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands for quotes, option chains, warrants, kline data, and help output. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes a local binary with user-provided market symbols and dates.
medium_findings.recommendation: Publish with a warning that the skill may run the Longbridge CLI. Runtime implementations should pass arguments without shell interpolation and validate symbols, dates, and OCC codes before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly document local CLI invocation patterns. Confidence is high that external command execution is intended, while malicious intent is not supported by the surrounding context.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts appear on financial options terminology and output templates, not cryptographic implementations. I found no hashing, encryption, signing, or random number generation code in the referenced lines.
low_findings.recommendation: Treat these alerts as scanner noise unless future versions add actual cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is readable Markdown about trading strategies and templates. There is no code path that performs cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive Environment and Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The environment access and reconnaissance alerts are caused by prose such as market environment, symbol resolution, and output table fields. No evidence found for process environment reads, host probing, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these alerts. Reassess if executable code is added that reads environment variables or enumerates the host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language workflow and financial analysis terms. There are no environment variable APIs or reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Longbridge CLI Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands for quotes, option chains, warrants, kline data, and help output. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes a local binary with user-provided market symbols and dates.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Publish with a warning that the skill may run the Longbridge CLI. Runtime implementations should pass arguments without shell interpolation and validate symbols, dates, and OCC codes before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly document local CLI invocation patterns. Confidence is high that external command execution is intended, while malicious intent is not supported by the surrounding context.

content.user_title: Analyze Options and Warrants with Longbridge
content.value_statement: Derivatives research often needs option chains, Greeks, IV, volume, and warrant data from one source. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Longbridge data for structured derivatives analysis.
actual_capabilities: Routes option quote, chain, volume, strategy, P&L, IV, and warrant requests to focused reference files.
Explains OCC option symbols and maps user inputs to quote or chain discovery workflows.
Guides analysis of Greeks, implied volatility, volume, open interest, and payoff metrics.
Supports Hong Kong warrant and CBBC quote, list, and issuer lookup workflows.
Provides templates for strategy selection, volatility snapshots, P&L summaries, and advanced options analysis.
limitations: Requires the Longbridge CLI or compatible MCP tools for live market data.
Does not place trades, manage orders, or provide personalized investment advice.
Accuracy depends on available Longbridge data, market access, and symbol liquidity.
Advanced calculations may be approximate when historical data or Greeks are missing.

use_cases.title: Review an option chain
use_cases.description: Compare expiries, strikes, IV, volume, and Greeks before selecting contracts for further research.

use_cases.title: Build a strategy snapshot
use_cases.description: Map a market view to candidate spreads, breakevens, max loss, max profit, and risk drivers.

use_cases.title: Screen Hong Kong warrants
use_cases.description: Find warrant quotes, issuer lists, leverage, premium, and IV for an underlying security.

prompt_templates.title: Check an option quote
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the AAPL 2024-01-19 190 call using Longbridge data. Show premium, IV, Greeks, volume, and open interest.

prompt_templates.title: Compare option chain expiries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge to compare TSLA option expiries for the next month. Summarize ATM IV, active strikes, and liquidity.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate strategy P&L
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a bull call spread example for MSFT using live Longbridge chain data. Include breakeven, max loss, max profit, and Greeks.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze volatility structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate NVDA implied volatility using Longbridge. Compare IV versus HV, describe skew, and suggest educational strategy candidates.

output_examples.input: Show a Longbridge volatility snapshot for TSLA options.
output_examples.output: A concise report with ATM IV, historical volatility estimate, IV richness, skew shape, and strategy signals. The response includes a non-advice disclaimer.

output_examples.input: Explain the P&L for a two-leg call spread.
output_examples.output: A position summary with each leg, payoff table, breakeven, maximum profit, maximum loss, and net Greeks in plain language.
A warrant research summary with available instruments, issuer context, leverage, premium, IV, and quote fields from Longbridge.

best_practices: Provide the symbol, market suffix, expiry, strike, and call or put direction when available.
Ask for educational analysis and verify live quotes before making financial decisions.
Use structured outputs that show source, assumptions, risks, and non-advice disclaimers.
anti_patterns: Do not treat example strategies as personalized investment advice.
Do not use stale quotes for fast-moving options or warrants.
Do not run symbols, dates, or OCC codes that include shell metacharacters.

faq.question: Does this skill place options or warrant trades?
faq.answer: No. It supports research and analysis only. It does not submit orders or manage positions.

faq.question: Which markets does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on US options and Hong Kong warrants through Longbridge data and tooling.

faq.question: Does it require a Longbridge login?
faq.answer: Basic option and warrant data is described as public, but US market access may be required.

faq.question: Can it analyze multi-leg strategies?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for spreads, straddles, strangles, covered calls, and volatility strategies.

faq.question: Can it calculate implied volatility signals?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides IV versus HV comparison, IV percentile estimates, volatility smile, and skew review.

faq.question: Is the output financial advice?
faq.answer: No. The skill frames outputs as educational research and includes investment risk disclaimers.
